commit.message:
Classify Pragma_NoTrailingNewline_NoError → §22.11 [skip ci]

The test exercises the `pragma compiler directive defined in §22.11. The pragma syntax (Syntax 22-8) defines the directive as `pragma pragma_name [...]. The test verifies that a pragma at end-of-file without a trailing newline is accepted without error, which is governed by the base pragma directive parsing rules in §22.11. §22.11.1 covers only named standard pragmas (reset, resetall, protect) and is not the relevant subclause.

commit.message:
Fix smoke-test hanging indefinitely on unresponsive backend

Added --max-time 10 to all curl calls. Without a timeout, a hung backend TLS
handshake causes the smoke test to block forever.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: GEMV grid 4→16 blocks/SM — restore Jetson occupancy from 25% to 100%

Five-Whys: 47e52e2 set grid cap to num_sms*4 (tuned for 4090's 128 SMs).
On Jetson (8 SMs) this gave 32 blocks → 4 blocks/SM → 12 warps → 25%
occupancy. The branch (gh-129) used 256 blocks → 16 active blocks/SM →
48 warps → 100% occupancy → 27.8 tok/s vs 14.2 tok/s with *4.

Fix: num_sms*16, matching max 48 warps/SM ÷ 3 warps/block = 16 blocks/SM.
- Jetson (8 SMs):  128 blocks → 16 blocks/SM → 48 warps → full occupancy
- 4090 (128 SMs): 2048 blocks → 16 blocks/SM → 48 warps → full occupancy

Refs PMAT-035

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix vocal classification with dedicated voice/instrumental model (#308)

The MTG-Jamendo instrument model's "voice" class (1 of 40 tags) was
unreliable for detecting vocals — often misclassifying them as drums,
bass, or synthesizer. Add a dedicated voice/instrumental classification
head (voice_instrumental-discogs-effnet-1.onnx, 96% accuracy) that

overrides the instrument head prediction.

commit.message:
fix: resolve tryke.expect module vs function name collision in worker

`import tryke.expect as _expect_mod` was getting the `expect` function
(re-exported in tryke/__init__.py) instead of the tryke.expect module,
causing AttributeError on SoftContext. Use sys.modules to bypass the
shadowed attribute.

commit.message:
Interagent Go rewrite (D091): single binary replaces Python+Bash daemon

cmd/interagent/ — Go 1.24+, Cobra CLI, 10 subcommands:
  serve:     HTTP daemon + scheduler + managed cloudflared subprocess
  tui:       Bubble Tea dashboard (3 tabs, mouse, scroll wheel, arrows,
             pgup/pgdn, clickable tabs, word-wrap)
  status:    one-shot CLI health check
  trigger:   manual prompt on a repo
  sessions:  list resumable Claude Code sessions
  resume:    resume most recent failed session
  pause:     activate kill switch
  unpause:   deactivate kill switch
  install:   auto-detect OS, generate launchd/systemd service files
  changelog: git log + sidecar JSONL → JSON for website builds

Persistence: SQLite WAL (modernc.org/sqlite, pure Go) — activity log,
sessions, sync timestamps, budget state all survive restarts.

Tunnel: managed cloudflared subprocess with auto-create on first install.
Single launchd plist replaces old two-plist (daemon+tunnel) setup.

Changelog pages: /changelog on both unratified.org and blog.unratified.org
— 26 entries from git history, commit hashes linked to GitHub, agent badge
on Co-Authored commits, cross-linked between sites.

Deployed: live on gray-box port 8787, cloudflared tunnel connected,
old Python plists unloaded. scripts/interagent-daemon/ kept as reference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify Pragma_NameTrailingWhitespace_NoError → §22.11 [skip ci]

Section 22.11 defines the `pragma directive syntax (Syntax 22-8), including that the pragma_name is optionally followed by pragma_expression items. The test exercises the case where nothing follows the pragma name except trailing whitespace — valid per the grammar since pragma_expression is optional. There is no deeper subclause covering this general syntax rule.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve_hostname endianness -- use to_ne_bytes() not to_be_bytes() (#21)

sceNetResolverStartNtoA stores the IP in network byte order (big-endian)
in memory. On little-endian MIPS (PSP), reading the in_addr as a u32
gives a byte-swapped value. Using to_be_bytes() then double-swaps,
producing reversed IPs (e.g., 207.241.224.2 becomes 2.224.241.207).

to_ne_bytes() returns the raw memory bytes, which are already in the
correct network byte order for sockaddr_in.

Co-authored-by: AI Agent Bot <ai-agent@localhost>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Return 429/502 on Gemini failures so frontend can react
- Add exponential backoff retry (3 attempts) on quota/rate-limit errors
- Fixes users being charged for quota-exhausted checks

Replace referral-based withdrawal requirements with global targets system

New requirements for withdrawals and reinvestments:
- Global deposits must reach 10,000 USDT (was: 2 L1 + 4 L2 referrals with Premium)
- At least 100 active users with Premium staking (was: personal referral counts)

Updated files:
- User.ts: Modified canWithdraw() and canReinvest() to check global stats
- WithdrawalService.ts: Updated error messages with global targets
- WithdrawForm.tsx, ReferralWithdrawForm.tsx: Updated UI messages
- UserDashboard.tsx: Updated reinvest requirements notice

commit.message:
Classify Pragma_MacroExpansionInName → §22.11 [skip ci]

The test exercises the `pragma compiler directive. §22.11 defines the pragma syntax (Syntax 22-8): pragma_name is a simple_identifier and pragma_value can be a number, string, or identifier. The test uses `MY_VAL (which expands to 42 per §22.2's rule that macro expansion occurs within compiler directives) as a pragma argument, producing a valid pragma_value (number). The most specific clause is §22.11, which governs the `pragma directive structure and argument grammar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rename NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL to APP_URL (server-side only)

Variable is only used in API routes — no need for the NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix. This also fixes Vercel env var saving.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden server: 12 resilience fixes

1. process.on uncaughtException/unhandledRejection to survive unexpected errors
2. try/catch around computeBotAction so a bot crash can't kill the server
3. match_card now checks matchWindowOpen before accepting a match
4. pendingBotTimers Set prevents scheduleBotTurnIfNeeded from stacking timeouts
5. Host transferred to next connected player when original host's 90s timer expires
6. Rooms auto-delete 10 min after game_over or empty lobby (memory leak fix)
7. targetScore clamped to 25-200 in steps of 25 server-side
8. Player names capped at 20 chars server-side in create_room and join_room
9. Watchdog auto-advances disconnected human's turn after 30 s idle
10. genRoomId retries on collision
11. Per-socket rate limit of 30 game-action events/sec via getRoomAndPlayer
12. Voice signaling (offer/answer/ice) requires sender and target in same room

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: enum plan_status_type incompleto — causa raíz del Error de conexión

El INSERT en /api/plans/generate fallaba porque 'generating' y 'error'
no existían en el enum plan_status_type de PostgreSQL (solo tenía
'draft', 'approved', 'sent'). Supabase devolvía un error de tipo
'invalid input value for enum' que se tragaba silenciosamente.

- migrations/005: ALTER TYPE plan_status_type ADD VALUE 'generating'/'error'
- route.ts: log de createError.code/message/details/hint antes del 500;
  incluye detail en el JSON de respuesta
- page.tsx: destructura plansError y loguea code/message/details
- generate-button.tsx: lee el body JSON del error HTTP y muestra detail
  real en lugar del texto genérico; log del error de red en el catch

commit.message:
perf: optimize OHIF viewer load time and nginx DICOM proxy

OHIF config:
- Increase web workers from 3 to navigator.hardwareConcurrency (auto)
- Enable SharedArrayBuffer (AUTO mode) for zero-copy pixel transfer
- Enable useNorm16Texture for GPU-native 16-bit CT rendering
- Enable preferSizeOverAccuracy for faster initial render

Nginx Orthanc proxy:
- Enable proxy_buffering with 32x256k buffers for DICOM pixel data
- Increase proxy_read_timeout to 300s for large series
- Add Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header for OHIF iframe

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add review management API, remove broken Avito scraper

Replace failed Avito scraping (blocked by CloudFlare/captcha) with
protected REST API endpoints for manual review management:
- GET /api/reviews — read current reviews
- POST /api/reviews/add — add single review (newest first, max 6)
- POST /api/reviews/set — replace all reviews at once
All write endpoints require REVIEWS_TOKEN for authentication.

Stability fixes:

- Use nest_asyncio when called from running event loop

Files fixed:
- scripts/import_claude_data.py (shell injection)
- bazinga/cli.py (4 bare excepts)
- bazinga/inter_ai.py (async context)
- bazinga/attestation_service.py (7 bare excepts)
